questions.question_text: An Alabama agent tells a buyer the roof is five years old when it is actually fifteen. What violation has occurred?
questions.explanation: Making false statements about property condition constitutes substantial misrepresentation under Section 34-27-36. Knowingly misstating the roof age is a direct false statement about condition, which is a specific statutory violation subject to disciplinary action by the Alabama Real Estate Commission.

questions.question_text: A licensee guarantees a buyer that rental income on an investment property will increase 10% annually. Which law does this violate?
questions.explanation: Alabama law prohibits licensees from guaranteeing future profits on real estate investments. Promising specific future income or appreciation amounts constitutes dishonest dealing under Section 34-27-36 because future market conditions cannot be guaranteed.

questions.question_text: An Alabama agent overstates a property's value by $50,000 to secure the listing. What type of violation is this?
questions.explanation: Intentionally misrepresenting a property's value to obtain a listing constitutes a false statement about value under Section 34-27-36. This is a form of substantial misrepresentation and dishonest dealing that subjects the licensee to disciplinary action by AREC.

questions.question_text: A broker purchases a listed property through a family member without disclosing her interest. What violation has occurred?
questions.explanation: When a licensee acquires a personal interest in a transaction without disclosure, they are acting as an undisclosed principal. Section 34-27-36 specifically prohibits acting as both agent and undisclosed principal. The broker must disclose any personal interest in the property.

questions.question_text: Which conduct is classified as dishonest dealing under Alabama license law?
questions.explanation: Guaranteeing future profits or property appreciation is specifically listed as dishonest dealing under Section 34-27-36. Recommending inspectors, maintaining commission rates, and referring to lenders are all permissible activities for Alabama licensees.

questions.question_text: A licensee convinces a buyer already under contract to cancel and purchase a different property listed by the licensee. What violation occurred?
questions.explanation: Alabama law prohibits inducing a party to break an existing contract for the purpose of substituting a new contract. This protects all parties involved in real estate transactions and prevents licensees from undermining existing agreements for personal gain.

questions.question_text: A listing agent receives three offers on Monday but only presents one to the seller on Tuesday. What violation occurred?
questions.correct_answer: Failure to present all offers promptly
questions.explanation: Alabama license law requires that all written offers be presented to the client promptly. Withholding offers or delaying presentation is a violation under Section 34-27-36. The agent must present all offers and let the seller decide which to accept, reject, or counter.

questions.question_text: An agent learns the property has foundation issues but tells the buyer only about cosmetic updates. Which violation applies?
questions.explanation: Failure to disclose known material defects is a violation of Alabama license law. Foundation issues are material facts that affect property value and safety. Licensees must disclose all known material facts to all parties regardless of whom they represent.

questions.question_text: A seller's agent persuades a buyer under contract with another seller to breach that contract. What is the primary violation?
questions.correct_answer: Inducing a party to break a contract
questions.explanation: Inducing a party to break an existing contract to substitute a new one is a specific violation under Section 34-27-36. This applies whether the licensee is working with a buyer or seller. The prohibition protects the integrity of existing contractual obligations.

questions.question_text: Which action by an Alabama licensee constitutes a disclosure violation?
distractors: Suggesting the buyer hire an inspector
questions.explanation: Failure to disclose known material defects is a specific violation under Alabama license law. Providing a CMA, suggesting inspections, and advertising accurate property information are all proper licensee activities that do not constitute violations.

questions.question_text: An Alabama licensee is sued by a client but does not notify the Commission. What requirement did the licensee violate?
questions.correct_answer: Duty to inform Commission of suits
distractors: Duty to file annual tax returns
questions.explanation: Alabama licensees are required to inform the Real Estate Commission of any lawsuit or judgment filed against them related to their real estate activities. Failure to report legal actions is itself a violation subject to disciplinary proceedings.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent receives a written offer from the seller but fails to give the buyer a copy. What violation occurred?
questions.explanation: Alabama law requires licensees to furnish copies of all documents to the appropriate parties. Failing to provide copies of offers, contracts, or other transaction documents is a violation under Section 34-27-36 classified as improper handling of documents.

questions.question_text: A listing agent receives two written offers simultaneously. One is from a close friend. What must the agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Present both offers promptly to the seller
questions.explanation: Alabama law mandates that all written offers must be presented promptly to the client. The agent cannot prioritize, withhold, or reject any offer regardless of personal relationships. The seller has the right to review all offers and decide how to proceed.

questions.question_text: When must an Alabama licensee inform the Commission about a court judgment?
questions.correct_answer: When the judgment relates to real estate activity
distractors: Only after completing the appeal process
Only if the broker requests it
questions.explanation: Alabama licensees must report any suit or judgment related to their real estate activities to the Commission. There is no minimum dollar threshold, and the obligation is not contingent on appeals or broker instruction. Failure to report is itself a disciplinary violation.

questions.question_text: Which document-handling practice violates Alabama license law?
questions.correct_answer: Failing to furnish transaction copies to parties
distractors: Storing copies in a digital filing system
questions.explanation: Failing to furnish copies of all transaction documents to the appropriate parties is a specific violation under Section 34-27-36. Using electronic signatures, digital storage, and certified mail are all acceptable document-handling practices under Alabama law.
